title: MLPS 2.0 GB/T 22239-2019 Level 3+ annual independent-assessment scheduling, MPS-accredited assessor freshness, and finding-remediation closure tracker
exclusions: Excludes Level 1 and Level 2 systems (covered by UC-22.61.1 grading + filing). Excludes systems formally decommissioned and de-registered with MPS.
evidence: Saved search `UC-22.61.11` archived daily to `audit_evidence` with `cn_csl_clause="MLPS-2-0-annual"`. Every row pins (system_id, mlps_grade, last_assessment_date, days_to_next_assessment, assessor_id, assessor_accreditation_expiry, open_findings_count, oldest_open_finding_days, risk_score).

compliance.assurance_rationale: GB/T 22239-2019 and MPS implementation regulations require that Level 3 systems be assessed annually, Level 4 semi-annually, and Level 5 quarterly, by an MPS-accredited testing organisation. Findings must be remediated within agreed timelines. This UC enforces both the scheduling cadence and the finding-remediation closure — the two operational limbs of the annual assessment obligation.
compliance.controlObjective: Demonstrates that Level 3+ systems are within their statutory assessment cadence with all findings remediated by the agreed due date.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search archived to `audit_evidence`, the `MLPS Assessment Health` dashboard, the per-system Cybersecurity Classified Protection Assessment Reports, the finding-remediation register.

compliance.assurance_rationale: GB/T 22239-2019 Level 3 requires both technical baseline controls and the ongoing assurance produced by the annual independent assessment. This UC tracks the operational assurance limb — the assessment is on schedule, findings are remediated — that complements the technical-baseline tracker in UC-22.61.1.
compliance.controlObjective: Operationalises GB/T 22239-2019 Level 3 ongoing-assurance via assessment-cadence + finding-remediation tracking.

controlTest.positiveScenario: Schedule a Level 4 system's semi-annual assessment 7 days from now (well past the 183-day cadence by 30 days). UC-22.61.11 MUST flag with risk_score >= 85.
controlTest.negativeScenario: Complete a Level 3 system's annual assessment 30 days before the 12-month anniversary, with no open findings, and ensure the MPS-accredited assessor's accreditation is current. UC-22.61.11 MUST record risk_score=0.

dataSources: KV Store `cn_mlps_register_lookup` (UC-22.61.1 register) extended with `assessor_id`, `assessor_accreditation_expiry`, `next_scheduled_assessment_date`. KV Store `cn_mlps_findings_lookup` (finding_id, system_id, finding_severity, finding_opened_at, remediation_due_at, remediation_closed_at, finding_status). KV Store `cn_mps_accredited_assessors_lookup` (assessor_id, assessor_name, accreditation_ref, accreditation_expiry, accreditation_scope).
app: Splunk Enterprise Security (Splunkbase 263), Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow (Splunkbase 1928), Splunk Common Information Model Add-on (Splunkbase 1621).
spl:
| inputlookup cn_mlps_register_lookup
| where mlps_grade IN ("L3","L4","L5")
| eval now_ts = now()
| eval days_since_assessment = if(isnotnull(last_assessment_date), round((now_ts - last_assessment_date) / 86400, 1), null())
| eval target_interval_days = case(mlps_grade = "L3", 365, mlps_grade = "L4", 183, mlps_grade = "L5", 92, 1=1, 365)
| eval days_to_next_due = target_interval_days - days_since_assessment
| eval days_to_scheduled = if(isnotnull(next_scheduled_assessment_date), round((next_scheduled_assessment_date - now_ts) / 86400, 1), null())
| eval cadence_breach = if(days_since_assessment > target_interval_days, 1, 0)
| eval no_assessment_scheduled = if(days_since_assessment > (target_interval_days * 0.9) AND isnull(next_scheduled_assessment_date), 1, 0)
| lookup cn_mps_accredited_assessors_lookup assessor_id OUTPUT accreditation_expiry
| eval assessor_lapsed = if(isnotnull(accreditation_expiry) AND accreditation_expiry < now_ts, 1, 0)
| join type=left system_id [
    search index=summary source=mlps_findings earliest=-2y
    | eval finding_age_days = round((now() - finding_opened_at) / 86400, 1)
    | eval finding_open = if(isnull(remediation_closed_at), 1, 0)
    | stats sum(finding_open) as open_findings_count, max(eval(if(finding_open=1, finding_age_days, 0))) as oldest_open_finding_days by system_id
  ]
| eval open_findings_count = coalesce(open_findings_count, 0)
| eval oldest_open_finding_days = coalesce(oldest_open_finding_days, 0)
| eval finding_remediation_overdue = if(open_findings_count > 0 AND oldest_open_finding_days > 90, 1, 0)
| eval risk_score = case(
    cadence_breach = 1 AND mlps_grade IN ("L4","L5"), 100,
    cadence_breach = 1, 90,
    assessor_lapsed = 1, 85,
    no_assessment_scheduled = 1, 70,
    finding_remediation_overdue = 1 AND oldest_open_finding_days > 180, 80,
    finding_remediation_overdue = 1, 60,
    1=1, 0)
| where risk_score > 0
| sort 0 -risk_score, +days_to_next_due
| table system_id mlps_grade last_assessment_date days_since_assessment target_interval_days days_to_next_due next_scheduled_assessment_date assessor_id assessor_lapsed open_findings_count oldest_open_finding_days risk_score
description: Reconciles every Level 3+ system in the MLPS register against the statutory assessment cadence (L3: 365d, L4: 183d, L5: 92d), the assessing organisation's MPS accreditation status, and the finding-remediation register. Flags four operational gaps: (i) cadence breached (assessment overdue), (ii) no assessment scheduled while approaching the cadence boundary, (iii) MPS-accredited assessor's accreditation expired or lapsed, (iv) finding remediation overdue (>90 days for high-severity findings).
value: MPS routinely conducts dual-track enforcement: (i) scheduling breaches (overdue assessments) and (ii) finding-remediation breaches (assessments completed but findings not closed). A Level 4 system that has missed two consecutive semi-annual assessments is a typical trigger for MPS administrative measures — warning, ordered remediation, public exposure (xinyong gongshi), suspension of operation. For state-owned-enterprise CIIOs, MPS notifications also reach SASAC and may influence executive-bonus and rotation decisions. The remediation-closure limb is what differentiates passive scheduling from genuine compliance.
implementation: (1) Extend `cn_mlps_register_lookup` with `assessor_id` + `next_scheduled_assessment_date`. (2) Maintain `cn_mlps_findings_lookup` from the assessment-platform exports — one row per finding with severity, due-date, and closure-date. (3) Maintain `cn_mps_accredited_assessors_lookup` from the MPS-published accreditation register (refreshed quarterly). (4) Schedule UC-22.61.11 daily at 06:00 Beijing time; route `risk_score >= 90` to the CISO + Board Audit Committee at severity P1.
visualization: (Panel 1) Active L3+ assessment cadence heat map. (Panel 2) Open findings by severity and age. (Panel 3) Assessor accreditation freshness. (Panel 4) Cadence-breach trend (12 months).

references.title: GB/T 22239-2019 - Information security technology - Baseline for classified protection of cybersecurity (MLPS 2.0)
MPS Public Information Network Security Inspection Notice (2018-2024 cycle)

knownFalsePositives:
1. **Assessment scheduled within grace window**: MPS routinely grants 30-90 day grace extensions at year-end for Q4-scheduled assessments; ensure `next_scheduled_assessment_date` reflects the granted extension and load `grace_ref` in `cn_mlps_register_lookup`. 2. **Assessor in renewal**: an MPS-accredited assessor in active accreditation renewal may have a brief expiry-overlap window; load `renewal_letter_ref` and treat as in-window for up to 90 days. 3. **Finding under regulator-approved deferred remediation**: a finding whose remediation has been formally deferred by the sectoral department (e.g., pending vendor patch) should carry `deferred_until` and be excluded from the remediation-overdue alert during the deferral.

**Suppression mechanism**: KV Store `cn_csl_22_61_11_exceptions` with the CISO documented approval, an explicit system_id + finding_id scope, an expiry date, and a written justification.
reviewer: PRC Cybersecurity SME Panel + CISO + Internal Audit
detailedImplementation:
## Prerequisites

- Splunk Enterprise >= 9.2 or Splunk Cloud (PRC region).
- UC-22.61.1 deployed with `cn_mlps_register_lookup` populated.
- `cn_mlps_findings_lookup` populated from the assessment platform.
- `cn_mps_accredited_assessors_lookup` curated quarterly from MPS publications.

## Step 1 - Assessor + finding KV Stores

```ini
[cn_mlps_findings_lookup]
enforceTypes = true
field.finding_id = string
field.system_id = string
field.finding_severity = string
field.finding_opened_at = time
field.remediation_due_at = time
field.remediation_closed_at = time
field.finding_status = string
field.deferred_until = time

[cn_mps_accredited_assessors_lookup]
enforceTypes = true
field.assessor_id = string
field.assessor_name = string
field.accreditation_ref = string
field.accreditation_expiry = time
field.accreditation_scope = string
```

## Step 2 - Findings summary index seed

Daily roll-up of `cn_mlps_findings_lookup` into `index=summary source=mlps_findings`.

## Step 3 - Schedule

```ini
[UC-22.61.11: MLPS Assessment Health]
cron_schedule = 0 6 * * *
dispatch.earliest_time = -1d@d
dispatch.latest_time = now
actions = summary_index, notable
action.summary_index._name = audit_evidence
action.summary_index.marker = uc=22.61.11,reg=CN-CSL,clause=MLPS-2-0-annual
alert.severity = 4
```

## Step 4 - PRC-specific notes

- **Joint inspections** (CAC + MPS + sectoral): the annual MLPS independent assessment may be folded into a joint inspection at the regulator's option; the joint inspection counts as the MLPS assessment provided the assessor on the team holds MPS accreditation.
- **MPS public exposure (xinyong gongshi)**: persistent non-compliance is published on the local public-security bureau website — managing this list is a board-level reputation risk.
grandmaExplanation: Important computer systems in China must be inspected by a government-approved tester every year. We make sure those inspections happen on time, that the testers are still approved, and that every problem they find is fixed quickly.
